summary: Despite three attempts (initial solver + two security fixer iterations), the code continues to trigger a CodeQL path-injection finding at line 45. The sanitize_filename function strips dots and slashes, but the SAST tool (and judge) flag the absence of an explicit path containment check (e.g., os.path.realpath comparison), leaving a UNCERTAIN verdict that prevents final security sign-off.
suggested_actions: Add an explicit path containment check after constructing filename: compute realpath of both the notes directory and the constructed path, then verify the constructed path starts with the notes directory realpath.
Alternatively, use a strict allowlist for filenames (e.g., only alphanumeric plus underscore) and reject any title that does not match.
Ensure the empty-title edge case is handled (e.g., reject empty title before constructing filename).
